#!/usr/bin/env python3
import unittest

import torch

import substrate
import substrate.language as S
from substrate.testing import has_rocm


@substrate.jit
def gemm_mfma_16x16x16(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.u32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.u32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    c_lane = S.full((4,), 0.0, S.f32)

    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16))

    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], c_lane)
    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(m_a[1], m_b[1], c_lane)

    C[lane] = c_lane


@substrate.jit
def gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    c_lane = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))

    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], c_lane)

    C[lane] = c_lane


@unittest.skipUnless(
    has_rocm(),
    "Requires ROCm/HIP with an AMD GPU.",
)
class TestGEMMSimple(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        """Set up test fixtures before each test method."""
        torch.manual_seed(0)
        self.M = 16
        self.N = 16
        self.K = 32
        self.WARP_SIZE = 64
        self.rtol = 1e-2
        self.atol = 1e-3

    def test_gemm_mfma_16x16x16(self):
        """Test simple GEMM with single MFMA operation (no tiling)."""
        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
            self.skipTest("CUDA is not available.")
        if not hasattr(torch.version, "hip") or torch.version.hip is None:
            self.skipTest("HIP is not available.")

        A = torch.randn((self.M, self.K), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        B = torch.randn((self.K, self.N), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        C = torch.zeros((self.M, self.N), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")

        expected = (A @ B).to(dtype=torch.float16, device="cpu")

        frag_size = self.K // 4  # mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32 loads 4 f16 elements

        A_shuffled = torch.zeros((self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(self.WARP_SIZE):
            i = lane % 16
            k_block = lane // 16
            for t in range(frag_size):
                k = k_block * frag_size + t
                A_shuffled[lane, t] = A[i, k]

        B_shuffled = torch.zeros((self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(self.WARP_SIZE):
            k_block = lane // 16
            j = lane % 16
            for t in range(frag_size):
                k = k_block * frag_size + t
                B_shuffled[lane, t] = B[k, j]

        A_packed = A_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size // 2)
        B_packed = B_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size // 2)
        C_shuffled = torch.zeros((self.WARP_SIZE, 4), dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")

        gemm_mfma_16x16x16[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (self.WARP_SIZE, 1, 1))](A_packed, B_packed, C_shuffled)

        for lane in range(self.WARP_SIZE):
            g = lane // 16
            j = lane % 16
            for t in range(4):
                C[4 * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t].to(torch.float16)

        actual = C.to("cpu")

        self.assertTrue(
            torch.allclose(actual, expected, rtol=self.rtol, atol=self.atol),
            msg=f"GEMM results do not match.\nExpected:\n{expected}\nActual:\n{actual}\n"
            f"Max absolute difference: {torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected))}",
        )

    def test_gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16(self):
        """Test GEMM with bf16 MFMA 32x32x8."""
        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
            self.skipTest("CUDA is not available.")
        if not hasattr(torch.version, "hip") or torch.version.hip is None:
            self.skipTest("HIP is not available.")

        m = 32
        n = 32
        k = 8
        warp_size = 64
        row_block = m // (warp_size // n)

        A = torch.randn((m, k), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        B = torch.randn((k, n), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        C = torch.zeros((m, n), dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")

        expected = (A @ B).to(dtype=torch.float32, device="cpu")

        frag_size = k // (warp_size // m)

        def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
            high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
            rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
            return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

        A_shuffled = torch.zeros((warp_size, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(warp_size):
            i = permute_row(lane % m)
            k_block = lane // m
            for t in range(frag_size):
                kk = k_block * frag_size + t
                A_shuffled[lane, t] = A[i, kk]

        B_shuffled = torch.zeros((warp_size, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(warp_size):
            k_block = lane // n
            j = lane % n
            for t in range(frag_size):
                kk = k_block * frag_size + t
                B_shuffled[lane, t] = B[kk, j]

        A_packed = A_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(warp_size, frag_size // 2)
        B_packed = B_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(warp_size, frag_size // 2)
        C_shuffled = torch.zeros((warp_size, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")

        gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (warp_size, 1, 1))](
            A_packed, B_packed, C_shuffled
        )

        for lane in range(warp_size):
            g = lane // n
            j = lane % n
            for t in range(row_block):
                C[row_block * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t]

        actual = C.to("cpu")

        self.assertTrue(
            torch.allclose(actual, expected, rtol=self.rtol, atol=self.atol),
            msg=f"GEMM results do not match.\nExpected:\n{expected}\nActual:\n{actual}\n"
            f"Max absolute difference: {torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected))}",
        )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main(verbosity=2)

Now I understand the correct lane mapping for `mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32`. Looking at lines 79-84 and 100-104:

For A matrix (input):
- `i = lane % 16` - row in A (0-15)
- `k_block = lane // 16` - which K block (0-3 for 64 lanes with frag_size=4 each)
- Each lane loads `A[i, k]` where `k = k_block * frag_size + t`

For B matrix (weight):
- `k_block = lane // 16`
- `j = lane % 16` - column in B (0-15)
- Each lane loads `B[k, j]` where `k = k_block * frag_size + t`

For C matrix (output):
- `g = lane // 16`
- `j = lane % 16`
- `C[4 * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t]`

So the output mapping is:
- Row: `4 * g + t = 4 * (lane // 16) + t` where t is 0-3 (the acc_idx)
- Col: `j = lane % 16`

This is DIFFERENT from what's in the current implementation! The current code has:
```python
row = tile_row_base + 16 * (acc_idx // 2) + 8 * (lane // 16) + (acc_idx % 2)
col = tile_col_base + (lane % 16)
```

But the correct mapping should be:
```python
row = tile_row_base + 4 * (lane // 16) + acc_idx
col = tile_col_base + (lane % 16)
```

Wait, let me double-check by looking at the test more carefully...

In the test:
- `g = lane // 16` ranges from 0-3 for lanes 0-63
- `j = lane % 16` ranges from 0-15


- `C[4 * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t]`

This means:
- lane 0: C[0, 0] = C_shuffled[0, t] for t=0,1,2,3 -> rows 0,1,2,3
- lane 15: C[0, 15] = C_shuffled[15, t] -> rows 0,1,2,3
- lane 16: C[4, 0] = C_shuffled[16, t] -> rows 4,5,6,7
- lane 31: C[4, 15] = C_shuffled[31, t] -> rows 4,5,6,7

- lane 63: C[12, 15] = C_shuffled[63, t] -> rows 12,13,14,15

So the correct output mapping is `row = tile_row_base + 4 * (lane // 16) + acc_idx` and `col = tile_col_base + (lane % 16)`. This differs significantly from the candidate_input.py pattern, which uses a more complex formula with `16 * (acc_idx // 2) + 8 * (lane // 16) + (acc_idx % 2)`.

That expression produces rows with gaps: for lane 0 it gives {0, 1, 16, 17}, for lane 16 it gives {8, 9, 24, 25}, for lane 32 it gives {16, 17, 32, 33}, and so on. This is clearly wrong compared to the test's expected pattern.

The correct formula should be `row = tile_row_base + 4 * (lane // 16) + acc_idx`, which produces contiguous blocks of rows for each lane group without gaps or overlaps.

I need to correct the row indexing across both the split_k_kernel and finalize_kernel. For the 32x32 tile structure, I should verify whether this requires 2 separate 16x16 tiles stacked vertically or horizontally. The current warp tiling with `warp_row * 64` and `tm * 32` suggests a 64x64 total, but I need to clarify how the 16x16 accumulators map into this structure. Each warp outputs a 64x64 region, which breaks down into 2x2 tiles of 32x32 elements each. However, this formula only generates rows 0-15, so I need to account for the tile structure properly. Looking at the test pattern where lanes 0-15 write rows 0-3, lanes 16-31 write rows 4-7, lanes 32-47 write rows 8-11, and lanes 48-63 continue the pattern, I can see how the lane groups map to row groups across the full 32x32 tile.

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import substrate
import substrate.language as S


BATCH = 8
IN_CHANNELS = 64
IN_H = 512
IN_W = 256
OUT_CHANNELS = 128
KERNEL_H = 5
KERNEL_W = 7
OUT_H = 508
OUT_W = 250
KERNEL_AREA = KERNEL_H * KERNEL_W
K_FLAT = IN_CHANNELS * KERNEL_AREA
M_FLAT = BATCH * OUT_H * OUT_W
BLOCK_M = 128
BLOCK_N = 128
WARP_TILE_M = 64
WARP_TILE_N = 64
WAVES_PER_BLOCK = 4

SPLIT_K_SLICES = 2
C_PER_SPLIT = (IN_CHANNELS + SPLIT_K_SLICES - 1) // SPLIT_K_SLICES
K_PER_SPLIT = C_PER_SPLIT * KERNEL_AREA

BF16_BYTES = 2
F32_BYTES = 4

INPUT0_SHAPE = (BATCH, IN_CHANNELS, IN_H, IN_W)
OUTPUT_SHAPE = (BATCH, OUT_CHANNELS, OUT_H, OUT_W)
WEIGHT_SHAPE = (OUT_CHANNELS, IN_CHANNELS, KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W)
WORKSPACE_SIZE = M_FLAT * OUT_CHANNELS


def _split_k_launch():
    grid_n = (OUT_CHANNELS + BLOCK_N - 1) // BLOCK_N
    grid_m = (M_FLAT + BLOCK_M - 1) // BLOCK_M
    return ((grid_n, grid_m, SPLIT_K_SLICES), (WAVES_PER_BLOCK * 64, 1, 1))


def _finalize_launch():
    grid_n = (OUT_CHANNELS + BLOCK_N - 1) // BLOCK_N
    grid_m = (M_FLAT + BLOCK_M - 1) // BLOCK_M
    return ((grid_n, grid_m, 1), (WAVES_PER_BLOCK * 64, 1, 1))


@substrate.jit
def split_k_kernel(
    X: S.Tensor((8, 64, 512, 256), S.f32),
    W: S.Tensor((128, 64, 5, 7), S.f16),
    workspace: S.Pointer(S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    warp_id = tid // 64
    lane = tid % 64

    warp_row = warp_id // 2
    warp_col = warp_id % 2

    tile_block_id = S.block_id(1)
    split_k_id = S.block_id(2)

    group_n_base = S.block_id(0) * 128
    group_m_base = tile_block_id * 128

    c_start = split_k_id * C_PER_SPLIT
    c_end = S.min(c_start + C_PER_SPLIT, IN_CHANNELS)

    lane_col = lane % 16
    lane_k_base = (lane // 16) * 4

    # Accumulator: 4 f32 values per 16x16 MFMA tile
    acc = S.full((2, 2, 4), 0.0, S.f32)

    k_iter_count = (c_end - c_start) * KERNEL_AREA
    k_tile_count = (k_iter_count + 3) // 4  # 4 K values per iteration

    zero_u32 = S.convert(0, S.u32)

    workspace_linear = S.make_tensor(
        workspace,
        S.f32,
        S.make_layout((M_FLAT * OUT_CHANNELS,), (1,)),
    )
    workspace_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(workspace_linear, WORKSPACE_SIZE * F32_BYTES)

    # Temporary f16 storage for MFMA
    a_f16 = S.full((2, 4), 0.0, S.f16)
    b_f16 = S.full((2, 4), 0.0, S.f16)

    for k_tile in S.range(k_tile_count):
        # Reset fragments
        for tm in S.range(2):
            for e in S.range(4):
                a_f16[tm, e] = S.convert(0.0, S.f16)
        for tn in S.range(2):
            for e in S.range(4):
                b_f16[tn, e] = S.convert(0.0, S.f16)

        k_base = k_tile * 4 + lane_k_base

        # Load A fragment
        for tm in S.range(2):
            m = group_m_base + warp_row * 64 + tm * 32 + lane_col
            if m < M_FLAT:
                batch = m // (OUT_H * OUT_W)
                out_idx = m % (OUT_H * OUT_W)
                o0 = out_idx // OUT_W
                o1 = out_idx % OUT_W

                for e in S.range(4):
                    k = k_base + e
                    if k < k_iter_count:
                        c = c_start + k // KERNEL_AREA
                        spatial = k % KERNEL_AREA
                        k0 = spatial // KERNEL_W
                        k1 = spatial % KERNEL_W
                        a_f16[tm, e] = S.convert(X[batch, c, o0 + k0, o1 + k1], S.f16)

        # Load B fragment (W is f16)
        for tn in S.range(2):
            n = group_n_base + warp_col * 64 + tn * 32 + lane_col
            if n < OUT_CHANNELS:
                for e in S.range(4):
                    k = k_base + e
                    if k < k_iter_count:
                        c = c_start + k // KERNEL_AREA
                        spatial = k % KERNEL_AREA
                        k0 = spatial // KERNEL_W
                        k1 = spatial % KERNEL_W
                        b_f16[tn, e] = W[n, c, k0, k1]

        # Execute MFMA - use f16 variant
        for tm in S.range(2):
            for tn in S.range(2):
                acc[tm, tn] = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(a_f16[tm], b_f16[tn], acc[tm, tn])

    for tm in S.range(2):
        tile_row_base = group_m_base + warp_row * 64 + tm * 32
        for tn in S.range(2):
            tile_col_base = group_n_base + warp_col * 64 + tn * 32
            col = tile_col_base + (lane % 16)
            if col < OUT_CHANNELS:
                for acc_idx in S.range(4):
                    row = tile_row_base + 16 * (acc_idx // 2) + 8 * (lane // 16) + (acc_idx % 2)
                    if row < M_FLAT:
                        linear_idx = row * OUT_CHANNELS + col
                        byte_offset = linear_idx * F32_BYTES
                        S.amdgpu.buffer_atomic_add_f32(acc[tm, tn, acc_idx], workspace_rsrc, byte_offset, zero_u32, 0)


@substrate.jit
def finalize_kernel(
    workspace: S.Pointer(S.f32),
    Y: S.Tensor((8, 128, 508, 250), S.bf16),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    warp_id = tid // 64
    lane = tid % 64

    warp_row = warp_id // 2
    warp_col = warp_id % 2

    group_n_base = S.block_id(0) * 128
    group_m_base = S.block_id(1) * 128

    lane_col = lane % 16

    workspace_matrix = S.make_tensor(
        workspace,
        S.f32,
        S.make_layout((M_FLAT, OUT_CHANNELS), (OUT_CHANNELS, 1)),
    )

    for tm in S.range(2):
        tile_row_base = group_m_base + warp_row * 64 + tm * 32
        for tn in S.range(2):
            tile_col_base = group_n_base + warp_col * 64 + tn * 32
            col = tile_col_base + lane_col
            if col < OUT_CHANNELS:
                for acc_idx in S.range(4):
                    row = tile_row_base + 16 * (acc_idx // 2) + 8 * (lane // 16) + (acc_idx % 2)
                    if row < M_FLAT:
                        batch = row // (OUT_H * OUT_W)
                        out_idx = row % (OUT_H * OUT_W)
                        o0 = out_idx // OUT_W
                        o1 = out_idx % OUT_W
                        val = workspace_matrix[row, col]
                        Y[batch, col, o0, o1] = S.convert(val, S.bf16)


class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels: int,
        out_channels: int,
        kernel_size,
        stride=(1, 1),
        padding=(0, 0),
        dilation=(1, 1),
        groups: int = 1,
        bias: bool = False,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        if isinstance(kernel_size, int):
            kernel_size = (kernel_size, kernel_size)
        if isinstance(stride, int):
            stride = (stride, stride)
        if isinstance(padding, int):
            padding = (padding, padding)
        if isinstance(dilation, int):
            dilation = (dilation, dilation)

        self.conv2d = nn.Conv2d(
            in_channels,
            out_channels,
            kernel_size,
            stride=stride,
            padding=padding,
            dilation=dilation,
            groups=groups,
            bias=bias,
        )
        self._cached_weight_ptr = None
        self._cached_weight_device = None
        self._cached_weight = None
        self._cached_workspace = None
        self._cached_workspace_device = None

    def _get_cached_weight(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        weight = self.conv2d.weight
        storage_ptr = weight.untyped_storage().data_ptr()
        device = x.device
        if (
            self._cached_weight is None
            or self._cached_weight_ptr != storage_ptr
            or self._cached_weight_device != device
        ):
            weight_dev = weight.detach().to(device=device, dtype=torch.float16).contiguous()
            self._cached_weight = weight_dev
            self._cached_weight_ptr = storage_ptr
            self._cached_weight_device = device
        return self._cached_weight

    def _get_cached_workspace(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        device = x.device
        if self._cached_workspace is None or self._cached_workspace_device != device:
            self._cached_workspace = torch.zeros(WORKSPACE_SIZE, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
            self._cached_workspace_device = device
        else:
            self._cached_workspace.zero_()
        return self._cached_workspace

    def forward(self, x):
        if tuple(x.shape) != INPUT0_SHAPE or x.dtype != torch.float32:
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports the benchmark input shape and dtype.")
        if (
            self.conv2d.in_channels != IN_CHANNELS
            or self.conv2d.out_channels != OUT_CHANNELS
            or self.conv2d.kernel_size != (KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W)
            or self.conv2d.stride != (1, 1)
            or self.conv2d.padding != (0, 0)
            or self.conv2d.dilation != (1, 1)
            or self.conv2d.groups != 1
            or self.conv2d.bias is not None
        ):
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports the benchmark convolution parameters.")

        w = self._get_cached_weight(x)
        workspace = self._get_cached_workspace(x)
        y = torch.empty(OUTPUT_SHAPE, device=x.device, dtype=torch.bfloat16)

        split_k_kernel[_split_k_launch](x, w, workspace.data_ptr())
        finalize_kernel[_finalize_launch](workspace.data_ptr(), y)

        return y

            stride=stride,
            padding=padding,
            dilation=dilation,
            groups=groups,
            bias=bias,
        )
        self._cached_weight_ptr = None
        self._cached_weight_device = None
        self._cached_weight = None
        self._cached_workspace = None
        self._cached_workspace_device = None

    def _get_cached_weight(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        weight = self.conv2d.weight
        storage_ptr = weight.untyped_storage().data_ptr()
        device = x.device
        if (
            self._cached_weight is None
            or self._cached_weight_ptr != storage_ptr
            or self._cached_weight_device != device
        ):
            weight_dev = weight.detach().to(device=device, dtype=torch.float16).contiguous()
            self._cached_weight = weight_dev
            self._cached_weight_ptr = storage_ptr
            self._cached_weight_device = device
        return self._cached_weight

    def _get_cached_workspace(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        device = x.device
        if self._cached_workspace is None or self._cached_workspace_device != device:
            self._cached_workspace = torch.zeros(WORKSPACE_SIZE, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
            self._cached_workspace_device = device
        else:
            self._cached_workspace.zero_()
        return self._cached_workspace

    def forward(self, x):
        if tuple(x.shape) != INPUT0_SHAPE or x.dtype != torch.float32:
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports the benchmark input shape and dtype.")
        if (
            self.conv2d.in_channels != IN_CHANNELS
            or self.conv2d.out_channels != OUT_CHANNELS
            or self.conv2d.kernel_size != (KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W)
            or self.conv2d.stride != (1, 1)
            or self.conv2d.padding != (0, 0)
            or self.conv2d.dilation != (1, 1)
            or self.conv2d.groups != 1
            or self.conv2d.bias is not None
        ):
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports the benchmark convolution parameters.")

        w = self._get_cached_weight(x)
        workspace = self._get_cached_workspace(x)
        y = torch.empty(OUTPUT_SHAPE, device=x.device, dtype=torch.bfloat16)

        split_k_kernel[_split_k_launch](x, w, workspace.data_ptr())
        finalize_kernel[_finalize_launch](workspace.data_ptr(), y)

        return y

def gemm_mfma_16x16x16(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.u32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.u32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    c_lane = S.full((4,), 0.0, S.f32)

    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16))

    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], c_lane)
    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(m_a[1], m_b[1], c_lane)

    C[lane] = c_lane


@substrate.jit
def gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    c_lane = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))

    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], c_lane)

    C[lane] = c_lane

Wait, the test K=32 is special because it needs 2 mfma calls. For K=16, it would only need 1 call.

Let me trace through more carefully:
- A_shuffled[lane, t] for lane 0-63, t 0-7
- For lane 0: i=0, k_block=0, loads A[0, 0..7]
- For lane 15: i=15, k_block=0, loads A[15, 0..7]
- For lane 16: i=0, k_block=1, loads A[0, 8..15]
- For lane 31: i=15, k_block=1, loads A[15, 8..15]
- For lane 32: i=0, k_block=2, loads A[0, 16..23]
- For lane 48: i=0, k_block=3, loads A[0, 24..31]

Then A_shuffled is packed as u32:
- A_packed = A_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(64, 4)
- This packs 8 f16 values into 4 u32 values

Then in the kernel:
- A[lane] gives 4 u32 values (8 f16 values)
- m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16)) gives shape (2, 4, 1)
- m_a[0] is shape (4, 1) - 4 f16 values for first K block (K 0-3 of that block's 8 values? No...)


- Each A[lane] contains 4 u32 = 8 f16, and when viewed as (2, 4, 1), the first dimension splits these into two groups of 4 f16 each. Each group gets processed by an MFMA operation: m_a[0] feeds the first instruction call and m_a[1] feeds the second. So each lane processes K indices across two separate 16x16x16 MFMA operations.

For K=16, the setup would differ. Looking at the test implementation, each lane extracts 8 consecutive K values from a row determined by `lane % 16`, with `frag_size = 8`. The K values span `k_block * frag_size + t` for t in [0,7], where `k_block = lane // 16`.

When packed as u32, these 8 f16 values condense to 4 u32. The kernel then reinterprets this as (2, 4, 1) shape, where m_a[0] contains the first 4 f16 values (K indices k_block*8 + [0..3]) and m_a[1] contains the next 4 (K indices k_block*8 + [4..7]). The first mfma call uses m_a[0] for K indices k_block*8 to k_block*8+3, while the second uses m_a[1] for the remaining indices.

In the conv kernel, I'm iterating through K tiles where each tile processes 4 consecutive K values. My current implementation builds a_f16 with shape (2, 4) to handle two output tiles, with lane_k_base = (lane // 16) * 4 providing the per-lane K offset. This seems consistent with how the test structures its K processing, though I should verify the lane indexing matches the expected pattern.

Looking at the test's A packing strategy, lane N extracts row N % 16 and K indices [(N // 16) * 8 + t]. This means lanes 0-15 target rows 0-15 with K starting at 0, while lanes 16-31 revisit those same rows but shift to K values beginning at 8. The pattern repeats for subsequent lane groups, creating a distributed mapping across the matrix dimensions.

For a simplified K=16 scenario, frag_size becomes 4. Each lane now carries 4 f16 values packed into 2 u32, yielding shape (1, 4, 1) when unpacked. With only a single K-block, the distribution pattern changes accordingly.

The row assignment follows `i = lane % 16`, distributing rows 0-15 across lanes 0-15, 16-31, 32-47, and 48-63 cyclically. Meanwhile, K values map as `k = (lane // 16) * 4 + t` where t ranges from 0 to 3, giving:
- Lanes 0-15 access K indices 0-3
- Lanes 16-31 access K indices 4-7
- Lanes 32-47 access K indices 8-11
- Lanes 48-59 access K indices 12-15

This assignment appears inverted compared to typical conventions. In a 16x16 matrix multiplication where A is 16x16 and B is 16x16, the row distribution across lanes seems nonstandard—multiple lane groups are assigned to the same row indices.

Looking at the actual test setup with K=32 and frag_size=8, the pattern becomes clearer. Each lane loads 8 consecutive K values for its assigned row, with the K starting point determined by lane position. The view operation then splits these 8 values into chunks of 4, which the two MFMA instructions process separately—one handling the first 4 K values and the other the remaining 4.

In my implementation, I'm loading 4 K values per lane through `a_f16[tm, e]`, which aligns with how the test structures its data for a single MFMA call. The indexing `k = k_tile * 4 + lane_k_base + e` should correctly distribute K values across lanes, matching the test's approach where consecutive values within each chunk map to consecutive positions in the fragment.

26	MFMA_ACC_SIZE = 16
27	WAVE_REPEAT_M = 2
28	WAVE_REPEAT_N = 2
29	WARP_TILE_M = WAVE_REPEAT_M * MFMA_M
30	WARP_TILE_N = WAVE_REPEAT_N * MFMA_N
31	THREADS = WARP_SIZE * NUM_WARPS
32	K_BLOCK_SLICES = GROUP_K // MFMA_K
33	LOAD_GROUP_ROWS = GROUP_M
34	LOAD_GROUP_COUNT = THREADS // LOAD_GROUP_ROWS
35	LOADS_PER_THREAD = K_BLOCK_SLICES // LOAD_GROUP_COUNT
36	BF16_BYTES = 2
37	F32_BYTES = 4
38	SLICE_VECTOR_BYTES = MFMA_K * BF16_BYTES
39	SPLIT_K_SLICES = 2
40	WRITEBACK_GROUP_ROWS = 32
41	WRITEBACK_GROUPS = 4
42	WRITEBACK_ROWS_PER_THREAD = 16
43	
44	SHM_TOTAL_WORDS = K_BLOCK_SLICES * GROUP_M * 4 + K_BLOCK_SLICES * GROUP_N * 4
45	A_SHM_WORDS = K_BLOCK_SLICES * GROUP_M * 4
46	B_SHM_WORDS = K_BLOCK_SLICES * GROUP_N * 4
47	INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE = 32
48	INPUT_TRANSPOSE_THREADS = 256
49	INPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD = 4
50	PIPELINE_SHM_WORDS = 2 * SHM_TOTAL_WORDS    # 2-stage pipeline
51	WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H = 64
52	WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W = 16
53	WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_THREADS = 256
54	OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE = 32
55	OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_THREADS = 256
56	OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD = 4
57	
58	SCHED_MASK_MFMA = 0x8
59	SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD = 0x20
60	SCHED_MASK_DS_READ = 0x100
61	SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE = 0x200
62	SCHED_MASK_S_BARRIER = 0x0

64	
65	def _compute_magic_u32_params(divisor: int) -> tuple[int, int]:
66	    """Compute host-side magic/shift for q = (mul_hi(magic, n) + n) >> shift."""
67	    if divisor <= 0 or divisor >= (1 << 32):
68	        raise ValueError(f"divisor must be in [1, 2^32) (got {divisor})")
69	
70	    shift = (divisor - 1).bit_length()
71	    if divisor & (divisor - 1) == 0:
72	        return 0, shift
73	
74	    magic = ((1 << (32 + shift)) // divisor) - (1 << 32) + 1
75	    return magic, shift
76

78	@substrate.jit
79	def hot_loop_scheduler():
80	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
81	
82	    for _ in S.range(8):
83	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_READ, 1, 0)
84	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
85	
86	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_S_BARRIER, 1, 0)
87	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
88	
89	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
90	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
91	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
92	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
93	
94	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
95	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
96	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
97	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
98	
99	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
100	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
101	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
102	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
103	
104	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
105	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
106	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
107	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
108	
109	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
110	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
111	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
112	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
113	
114	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
115	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
116	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
117	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
118	
119	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
120	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
121	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
122	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 2, 0)
123	
124	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_WRITE, 1, 0)
125	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 1, 0)
126	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_BUFFER_LOAD, 1, 0)
127	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_MFMA, 3, 0)
128	
129	    # barrier for hacky LLVM
130	    S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_S_BARRIER, 1, 0)
131	
132	    for _ in S.range(8):
133	        S.amdgpu.sched_group_barrier(SCHED_MASK_DS_READ, 1, 0)

137	@substrate.jit
138	def _mdiv_u32(numer: S.u32, magic: S.u32, shift: S.u32) -> S.u32:
139	    prod_hi = S.convert((S.convert(magic, S.u64) * S.convert(numer, S.u64)) >> 32, S.u32)
140	    return (prod_hi + numer) >> shift
141	
142	
143	@substrate.jit
144	def _mdiv_u32_rem(
145	    numer: S.u32,
146	    denom: S.u32,
147	    magic: S.u32,
148	    shift: S.u32,
149	) -> (S.u32, S.u32):
150	    quot = _mdiv_u32(numer, magic, shift)
151	    rem = numer - quot * denom
152	    return quot, rem
153

155	def _igemm_launch_config(batch_size, out_channels, out_h, out_w):
156	    """Compute grid dimensions for the MFMA implicit-GEMM kernel."""
157	    gemm_m = batch_size * out_h * out_w
158	    gemm_n = out_channels
159	
160	    m_groups = (gemm_m + GROUP_M - 1) // GROUP_M
161	    n_groups = (gemm_n + GROUP_N - 1) // GROUP_N
162	
163	    grid = (m_groups * n_groups * SPLIT_K_SLICES, 1, 1)
164	    block = (THREADS, 1, 1)
165	    return grid, block
166

168	def _store_output_launch_config(numel):
169	    """Compute grid dimensions for the tiled fp32->bf16 output transpose."""
170	    batch_size, out_channels, out_h, out_w = numel
171	    hw_out = out_h * out_w
172	    hw_tiles = (hw_out + OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
173	    channel_tiles = (out_channels + OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
174	    grid = (batch_size * hw_tiles * channel_tiles, 1, 1)
175	    block = (OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_THREADS, 1, 1)
176	    return grid, block

179	def _transpose_input_launch_config(batch_size, in_channels, in_h, in_w):
180	    """Compute grid dimensions for the tiled NCHW->NHWC input transpose."""
181	    hw_in = in_h * in_w
182	    h_tiles = (in_channels + INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
183	    w_tiles = (hw_in + INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
184	    grid = (batch_size * h_tiles * w_tiles, 1, 1)
185	    block = (INPUT_TRANSPOSE_THREADS, 1, 1)
186	    return grid, block

189	def _transpose_weight_launch_config(out_channels, in_channels, kernel_h, kernel_w):
190	    """Compute grid dimensions for the tiled OIHW->OHWI weight transpose."""
191	    kernel_area = kernel_h * kernel_w
192	    h_tiles = (in_channels + WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H - 1) // WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H
193	    w_tiles = (kernel_area + WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W - 1) // WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W
194	    grid = (out_channels * h_tiles * w_tiles, 1, 1)
195	    block = (WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_THREADS, 1, 1)
196	    return grid, block

199	@substrate.jit
200	def _compute_input_addr(
201	    a_row: S.u32,
202	    k_base: S.u32,
203	    c_start: S.u32,
204	    split_channels: S.u32,
205	    hw_out: S.u32,
206	    out_w: S.u32,
207	    in_h: S.u32,
208	    in_w: S.u32,
209	    in_channels: S.u32,
210	    kernel_w: S.u32,
211	    pad_h: S.u32,
212	    pad_w: S.u32,
213	    stride_h: S.u32,
214	    stride_w: S.u32,
215	    dilation_h: S.u32,
216	    dilation_w: S.u32,
217	    hw_out_magic: S.u32,
218	    hw_out_shift: S.u32,
219	    out_w_magic: S.u32,
220	    out_w_shift: S.u32,
221	    split_channels_magic: S.u32,
222	    split_channels_shift: S.u32,
223	    kernel_w_magic: S.u32,
224	    kernel_w_shift: S.u32,
225	) -> (S.u32, S.u32):
226	    """Compute the base address and offset for loading a staged input vector."""
227	    batch, hw_idx = _mdiv_u32_rem(a_row, hw_out, hw_out_magic, hw_out_shift)
228	    h_out_idx, w_out_idx = _mdiv_u32_rem(hw_idx, out_w, out_w_magic, out_w_shift)
229	    spatial, c_offset = _mdiv_u32_rem(
230	        k_base, split_channels, split_channels_magic, split_channels_shift
231	    )
232	    kh, kw = _mdiv_u32_rem(spatial, kernel_w, kernel_w_magic, kernel_w_shift)
233	    h = h_out_idx * stride_h - pad_h + kh * dilation_h
234	    w = w_out_idx * stride_w - pad_w + kw * dilation_w
235	    input_base = (((batch * in_h + h) * in_w + w) * in_channels) * BF16_BYTES
236	    input_off = (c_start + c_offset) * BF16_BYTES
237	    return input_base, input_off
238

240	@substrate.jit
241	def _transpose_input_nchw_to_nhwc_kernel(
242	    input_nchw: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
243	    input_nhwc: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
244	    batch_size: S.u32,
245	    in_channels: S.u32,
246	    in_h: S.u32,
247	    in_w: S.u32,
248	):
249	    """Transpose the input from NCHW to NHWC using 32x32 shared-memory tiles."""
250	    hw_in = in_h * in_w
251	    h_tiles = (in_channels + INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
252	    w_tiles = (hw_in + INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
253	
254	    linear_block_id = S.block_id(0)
255	    tiles_per_batch = h_tiles * w_tiles
256	    batch = linear_block_id // tiles_per_batch
257	    tile_id = linear_block_id - batch * tiles_per_batch
258	    tile_h = tile_id // w_tiles
259	    tile_w = tile_id % w_tiles
260	
261	    tid = S.thread_id(0)
262	    local_col = tid & 31
263	    local_row_base = (tid >> 5) * INPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD
264	    global_row_base = tile_h * INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + local_row_base
265	    global_col = tile_w * INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + local_col
266	
267	    src = S.make_tensor(
268	        input_nchw,
269	        S.bf16,
270	        S.make_layout(
271	            (batch_size, in_channels, hw_in),
272	            (in_channels * hw_in, hw_in, 1),
273	        ),
274	    )
275	    dst = S.make_tensor(
276	        input_nhwc,
277	        S.bf16,
278	        S.make_layout(
279	            (batch_size, hw_in, in_channels),
280	            (hw_in * in_channels, in_channels, 1),
281	        ),
282	    )
283	    tile = S.make_shared((INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE, INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + 1), S.bf16)
284	
285	    for i in S.range(INPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD):
286	        src_row = global_row_base + i
287	        if batch < batch_size and src_row < in_channels and global_col < hw_in:
288	            tile[local_row_base + i, local_col] = src[batch, src_row, global_col]
289	    S.syncthreads()
290	
291	    for i in S.range(INPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD):
292	        dst_row = tile_w * INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + local_row_base + i
293	        dst_col = tile_h * INPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + local_col
294	        if batch < batch_size and dst_row < hw_in and dst_col < in_channels:
295	            dst[batch, dst_row, dst_col] = tile[local_col, local_row_base + i]
296

298	@substrate.jit
299	def _transpose_weight_oihw_to_ohwi_kernel(
300	    weight_oihw: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
301	    weight_ohwi: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
302	    out_channels: S.u32,
303	    in_channels: S.u32,
304	    kernel_h: S.u32,
305	    kernel_w: S.u32,
306	):
307	    """Transpose weights from OIHW to OHWI using a 16x64 tile without widening bf16."""
308	    kernel_area = kernel_h * kernel_w
309	    h_tiles = (in_channels + WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H - 1) // WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H
310	    w_tiles = (kernel_area + WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W - 1) // WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W
311	
312	    linear_block_id = S.block_id(0)
313	    tiles_per_filter = h_tiles * w_tiles
314	    out_channel = linear_block_id // tiles_per_filter
315	    tile_id = linear_block_id - out_channel * tiles_per_filter
316	    tile_h = tile_id // w_tiles
317	    tile_w = tile_id % w_tiles
318	
319	    tid = S.thread_id(0)
320	    local_col = tid & 15
321	    local_row_pair = tid >> 4
322	    src_col = tile_w * WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W + local_col
323	    src_row_base = tile_h * WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H + local_row_pair * 2
324	
325	    src = S.make_tensor(
326	        weight_oihw,
327	        S.bf16,
328	        S.make_layout(
329	            (out_channels, in_channels, kernel_area),
330	            (in_channels * kernel_area, kernel_area, 1),
331	        ),
332	    )
333	    dst = S.make_tensor(
334	        weight_ohwi,
335	        S.bf16,
336	        S.make_layout(
337	            (out_channels, kernel_area, in_channels),
338	            (kernel_area * in_channels, in_channels, 1),
339	        ),
340	    )
341	    tile = S.make_shared((WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H, WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W + 1), S.bf16)
342	
343	    for i in S.range(2):
344	        src_row = src_row_base + i
345	        if out_channel < out_channels and src_row < in_channels and src_col < kernel_area:
346	            tile[local_row_pair * 2 + i, local_col] = src[out_channel, src_row, src_col]
347	
348	        src_row_hi = src_row_base + 32 + i
349	        if out_channel < out_channels and src_row_hi < in_channels and src_col < kernel_area:
350	            tile[local_row_pair * 2 + 32 + i, local_col] = src[out_channel, src_row_hi, src_col]
351	    S.syncthreads()
352	
353	    dst_row = tile_w * WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_W + local_col
354	    dst_col_base = tile_h * WEIGHT_TRANSPOSE_TILE_H + local_row_pair * 4
355	    for i in S.range(4):
356	        dst_col = dst_col_base + i
357	        if out_channel < out_channels and dst_row < kernel_area and dst_col < in_channels:
358	            tile_row = local_row_pair * 4 + i
359	            dst[out_channel, dst_row, dst_col] = tile[tile_row, local_col]
360

362	@substrate.jit
363	def _igemm_kernel(
364	    input_nhwc: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
365	    weight_ohwi: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
366	    out_accum: S.Pointer(S.f32),
367	    in_h: S.u32,
368	    in_w: S.u32,
369	    batch_size: S.u32,
370	    out_channels: S.u32,
371	    in_channels: S.u32,
372	    out_h: S.u32,
373	    out_w: S.u32,
374	    kernel_h: S.u32,
375	    kernel_w: S.u32,
376	    pad_h: S.u32,
377	    pad_w: S.u32,
378	    stride_h: S.u32,
379	    stride_w: S.u32,
380	    dilation_h: S.u32,
381	    dilation_w: S.u32,
382	    groups: S.u32,
383	    n_groups_magic: S.u32,
384	    n_groups_shift: S.u32,
385	    hw_out_magic: S.u32,
386	    hw_out_shift: S.u32,
387	    out_w_magic: S.u32,
388	    out_w_shift: S.u32,
389	    split_channels_magic: S.u32,
390	    split_channels_shift: S.u32,
391	    kernel_w_magic: S.u32,
392	    kernel_w_shift: S.u32,
393	):
394	    """Implicit-GEMM kernel tiled with 32x32x8 BF16 MFMA instructions."""
395	    gemm_m = batch_size * out_h * out_w
396	    gemm_n = out_channels
397	    gemm_k = in_channels * kernel_h * kernel_w
398	    kernel_area = kernel_h * kernel_w
399	    hw_out = out_h * out_w
400	
401	    linear_block_id = S.block_id(0)
402	    split_k_slices = SPLIT_K_SLICES
403	    n_groups = (gemm_n + GROUP_N - 1) // GROUP_N
404	    split_k_id = linear_block_id & (split_k_slices - 1)
405	    tile_block_id = linear_block_id >> 1
406	    group_m, group_n = _mdiv_u32_rem(tile_block_id, n_groups, n_groups_magic, n_groups_shift)
407	
408	    tid = S.thread_id(0)
409	    wid = tid // WARP_SIZE
410	    lane = tid % WARP_SIZE
411	    warp_row = wid // WARPS_N
412	    warp_col = wid % WARPS_N
413	    lane_col = lane % MFMA_N
414	    group_m_base = group_m * GROUP_M
415	    group_n_base = group_n * GROUP_N
416	
417	    input_linear = S.make_tensor(
418	        input_nhwc,
419	        S.bf16,
420	        S.make_layout((batch_size * in_h * in_w * in_channels,), (1,)),
421	    )
422	    weight_linear = S.make_tensor(
423	        weight_ohwi,
424	        S.bf16,
425	        S.make_layout((out_channels * kernel_area * in_channels,), (1,)),
426	    )
427	    out_accum_linear = S.make_tensor(
428	        out_accum,
429	        S.f32,
430	        S.make_layout((gemm_m * gemm_n,), (1,)),
431	    )
432	
433	    zero_f32 = S.convert(0.0, S.f32)
434	    zero_u32 = S.convert(0, S.u32)
435	    one_u32 = S.convert(1, S.u32)
436	
437	    a_shm_pair_layout = S.make_layout(
438	        (2, K_BLOCK_SLICES, GROUP_M, 2, 2),
439	        (A_SHM_WORDS, GROUP_M * 4, 4, 2, 1),
440	    )
441	    b_shm_pair_layout = S.make_layout(
442	        (2, K_BLOCK_SLICES, GROUP_N, 2, 2),
443	        (B_SHM_WORDS, GROUP_N * 4, 4, 2, 1),
444	    )
445	
446	    acc = S.make_local((WAVE_REPEAT_M, WAVE_REPEAT_N, MFMA_ACC_SIZE), S.f32)
447	    warmup_acc = S.make_local((2, MFMA_ACC_SIZE), S.f32)
448	    a_frag_words = S.make_local((2, WAVE_REPEAT_M, 2), S.u32)
449	    b_frag_words = S.make_local((2, WAVE_REPEAT_N, 2), S.u32)
450	    prefetch_a = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD, 4), S.u32)
451	    prefetch_b = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD, 4), S.u32)
452	    next_slice_k_base = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
453	    next_input_base = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
454	    next_input_off = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
455	    next_weight_off = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
456	    next_c_offset = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
457	    next_kw = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
458	    next_valid_col = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
459	    next_valid_row = S.make_local((LOADS_PER_THREAD,), S.u32)
460	    zero_vec = S.make_local((4,), S.u32)
461	    packed_a_word_layout = S.make_layout(
462	        (2, K_BLOCK_SLICES * GROUP_M, 4),
463	        (A_SHM_WORDS, 4, 1),
464	    )
465	    packed_b_word_layout = S.make_layout(
466	        (2, K_BLOCK_SLICES * GROUP_N, 4),
467	        (B_SHM_WORDS, 4, 1),
468	    )
469	    writeback_vec_layout = S.make_layout((SHM_TOTAL_WORDS // 4, 4), (4, 1))
470	    shared_words = S.make_shared((PIPELINE_SHM_WORDS,), S.u32)
471	    a_shm_storage = S.subview(shared_words, (0,), (2 * A_SHM_WORDS,), (1,))
472	    b_shm_storage = S.subview(

861	@substrate.jit
862	def _store_splitk_output_kernel(
863	    out_accum: S.Pointer(S.f32),
864	    out: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
865	    batch_size: S.u32,
866	    out_channels: S.u32,
867	    out_h: S.u32,
868	    out_w: S.u32,
869	):
870	    """Transpose the GEMM-major Split-K workspace into NCHW bf16 output."""
871	    hw_out = out_h * out_w
872	    hw_tiles = (hw_out + OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
873	    channel_tiles = (out_channels + OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE - 1) // OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE
874	
875	    linear_block_id = S.block_id(0)
876	    tiles_per_batch = hw_tiles * channel_tiles
877	    batch = linear_block_id // tiles_per_batch
878	    tile_id = linear_block_id - batch * tiles_per_batch
879	    tile_hw = tile_id // channel_tiles
880	    tile_channel = tile_id % channel_tiles
881	
882	    tid = S.thread_id(0)
883	    local_col = tid & 31
884	    local_row_base = (tid >> 5) * OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD
885	    src_hw_base = tile_hw * OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + local_row_base
886	    src_channel = tile_channel * OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + local_col
887	
888	    out_accum_matrix = S.make_tensor(
889	        out_accum,
890	        S.f32,
891	        S.make_layout((batch_size * hw_out, out_channels), (out_channels, 1)),
892	    )
893	    out_tensor = S.make_tensor(
894	        out,
895	        S.bf16,
896	        S.make_layout(
897	            (batch_size, out_channels, hw_out),
898	            (out_channels * hw_out, hw_out, 1),
899	        ),
900	    )
901	    tile = S.make_shared((OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE, OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_TILE + 1), S.bf16)
902	
903	    for i in S.range(OUTPUT_TRANSPOSE_ROWS_PER_THREAD):
904	        src_hw = src_hw_base + i
905	        if batch < batch_size and src_hw < hw_out and src_channel < out_channels:
906	            tile[local_row_base + i, local_col] = S.convert(
907	                out_accum_matrix[batch * hw_out + src_hw, src_channel], S.bf16
908	            )
909	    S.syncthreads()
910
